name: Katla
eruptionHistory: Has erupted roughly every 40-80 years. The last major eruption was in 1918. It is overdue by historical standards and closely monitored.
description: Katla is one of Iceland's most powerful and feared volcanoes. It lies beneath the Mýrdalsjökull glacier, and eruptions produce massive glacial floods (jökulhlaups) that reshape the landscape.
